name: windows-privacy

shell_commands.disables: Recall — disable AI data analysis (screenshots every few seconds on Win11 24H2+)
Recall — feature-gate off at machine level (deletes snapshots on reboot)
shell_commands.ui_path: Group Policy → Computer Configuration → Administrative Templates → Windows Components → Windows AI

shell_commands.disables: Legacy Windows Copilot (pre-chat-app experience). Note path is WindowsCopilot, not WindowsAI.
Windows diagnostic data (Enterprise/Education/Server: sets to Security; Home: value 0 unsupported, effective floor is Required [1])
shell_commands.ui_path: Settings → Privacy & security → Diagnostics & feedback → Diagnostic data
Settings → Privacy & security → General → Advertising ID

shell_commands.disables: Tailored experiences — 'Let Windows improve Start and search results'
shell_commands.ui_path: Settings → Privacy & security → Diagnostics & feedback → Tailored experiences

shell_commands.disables: Inking & Typing personalization (stops local keystroke model training)
shell_commands.ui_path: Settings → Privacy & security → Inking & typing personalization

notes: Recall has dual-key control: HKCU DisableAIDataAnalysis (user) + HKLM AllowRecallEnablement (machine, admin, deletes snapshots on reboot).
New Copilot chat app (post-2024 rebuild) is not covered by TurnOffWindowsCopilot — Microsoft's documented path is uninstall only.
Edge telemetry is out of scope — configure via Edge's own policy keys.

caveats.framing: On Windows Home, AllowTelemetry=0 is silently bumped to 1 (Required). Effective floor is Required. Confirm the target machine is Pro / Enterprise / Education before expecting Security-level off.
